title: Obione amboensis
taxonomy: Obione amboensis is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family Polygonaceae. It is a dioecious herb native to the Andes mountain range in Peru. Obione amboensis is placed in the genus Obione, which is a group of plants commonly known as sea purslanes or saltbushes. The classification of Obione amboensis is within the family Polygonaceae, which comprises over 2,000 species of plants, including buckwheat, rhubarb, and knotweed. The species is also classified as a member of the order Polygonales.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues.
